ctxMarket: Attention is on Solana’s payments infrastructure and new token launches, with record stablecoin volumes reported for February. SOL outperformed major peers, leading the CoinDesk 20 index higher.
ctxTalking:
• Launch of $BANK token on Solana, targeting on-chain poker staking
• Solana stablecoin volume hitting $650 billion in February, driven by payments demand
• Backpack and Superstate enabling IPO share purchases directly on Solana
ctxMatters: Solana’s growing stablecoin volume and payments activity highlight its traction with retail and institutional users. New token launches and tokenized IPO offerings suggest expanding use cases beyond DeFi and memecoins. The collapse of Step Finance after a wallet breach underscores ongoing security risks in the ecosystem.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Record stablecoin volume and new real-world asset tokenization point to sustained infrastructure adoption.
Noise: Short-term price volatility speculation lacks clear impact on Solana’s broader ecosystem trends.
ctxGlossary:
Stablecoin — A cryptocurrency pegged to a stable asset, often used for payments and transfers.
Tokenization — Converting real-world assets, like IPO shares, into blockchain-based tokens.

readMore.title: $BANK Sale Begins on Solana, Targeting Poker Staking Market
Morgan Stanley taps Coinbase and BNY for Bitcoin ETF custody
Backpack taps Superstate for IPO roadshow tokenization offering, opens waitlist
